cities.name: Kyoto
cities.description: Japan's ancient imperial capital — 17 UNESCO World Heritage sites, 1,600 Buddhist temples, and some of the world's best kaiseki cuisine packed into a single city.

pois.name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
pois.description: 10,000 vermilion torii gates winding 4 km up Mount Inari. Free entry, open 24 hrs.
pois.notes: Go at dawn — summit is deserted, lower gates are packed by 09:00

pois.name: Nishiki Market
pois.description: 'Kyoto's kitchen' — 400-year-old covered arcade with pickles, tofu, skewered delicacies.
pois.notes: Most stalls close by 18:00. Try the yuba (tofu skin) on skewers.

pois.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
pois.description: Dense bamboo corridor flanked by monkey park and Tenryū-ji Zen garden.
pois.notes: Take the Sagano Scenic Railway back — views of Hozu Gorge

pois.name: Pontocho Alley
pois.description: Narrow lantern-lit alley with riverside kaiseki restaurants — geisha sightings at dusk.
pois.notes: Reserve a window seat at a kawa-yuka terrace restaurant in summer

pois.name: Kyoto Station
pois.description: Futuristic 15-storey station hub. Shinkansen to Tokyo (2h15) and Osaka (15 min).
pois.notes: Ramen Koji on B1 floor — 9 ramen shops, great for a quick departure meal

cities.name: Marrakech
cities.description: The Red City — a labyrinth of medieval souks, riads, and spice markets at the edge of the Sahara. Every alley leads somewhere unexpected.

pois.name: Jemaa el-Fna Square
pois.description: UNESCO-listed living culture space — snake charmers by day, food stalls and storytellers by night.
pois.notes: The square transforms completely after sunset. Climb a café rooftop for the panorama.

pois.name: Souk Ahel Fes (Spice Square)
pois.description: Ras el hanout, saffron, argan oil — buy direct from the merchants who blend their own mixes.
pois.notes: Negotiate everything. First price is 3–5× the final price.

pois.name: Jardin Majorelle
pois.description: Yves Saint Laurent's electric-blue villa surrounded by cacti and exotic plants.
pois.notes: Buy tickets online — long queues otherwise. Berber Museum inside is worth it.

pois.name: Foundouk Restaurant
pois.description: 17th-century caravanserai converted into a multi-floor restaurant with live Gnawa music.
pois.notes: Ask for a table on the top floor. Pastilla (pigeon pie) is the signature dish.

pois.name: CTM Bus Terminal
pois.description: Main hub for intercity buses — Agadir (4h), Fes (8h), desert tours to Merzouga (9h).
pois.notes: Book CTM seats online — much safer than grand taxis for long hauls

cities.name: Lisbon
cities.description: Europe's westernmost capital — seven hills draped in azulejo tiles, melancholic fado drifting from open windows, and the Atlantic just beyond the bridge.

pois.name: Alfama District
pois.description: Lisbon's oldest neighbourhood — Moorish alleys, miradouros (viewpoints), and São Jorge Castle.
pois.notes: Wear flat shoes. The cobblestones are brutal. Best viewpoint: Portas do Sol.

pois.name: LX Factory Market
pois.description: Sunday market in a converted 19th-century industrial complex — craft food, vinyl, vintage.
pois.notes: Sunday only. The Heim Café croissants are worth queuing for.

pois.name: Tram 28 Ride
pois.description: Vintage yellow tram grinding through the steepest streets of Graça, Alfama, and Baixa.
pois.notes: Board at Martim Moniz to avoid the worst crowds. Watch your pockets.

pois.name: Tasca do Chico
pois.description: 30-seat fado house in Bairro Alto — intimate performances, no tourist-trap atmosphere.
pois.notes: Reservations essential, book weeks ahead. Petiscos (small plates) only.

pois.name: Lisboa Oriente Station
pois.description: Calatrava-designed station — Alfa Pendular trains to Porto (3h) and Algarve (3.5h).
pois.notes: Comboios de Portugal app for tickets. Buy at least a day ahead for Alfa Pendular.

cities.name: Oaxaca
cities.description: Mexico's culinary and cultural heartland — mole negro, mezcal distilleries, Zapotec ruins at Monte Albán, and the world's most vivid Día de los Muertos celebration.

pois.name: Monte Albán
pois.description: Zapotec mountain-top city from 500 BCE — grand plazas, ball court, and sweeping valley views.
pois.notes: Take a colectivo from Calle Trujano. Morning light is best for photos.

pois.name: Mercado de la Merced
pois.description: Local market for tlayudas, memelas, and the 7 classic Oaxacan moles. No tourists.
pois.notes: Comida corrida (set lunch) stalls open from 13:00. Cheapest mole negro in the city.

pois.name: Mezcal Distillery Tour (Papalometl)
pois.description: Third-generation palenque using clay pot distillation — see agave roasting pits and taste 5 expressions.
pois.notes: Hire a taxi for the day — the best palenques are 45 min outside the city in Matatlán.

pois.name: La Olla de Carmen
pois.description: Hole-in-the-wall run by one woman — only mole negro and black bean soup, tables in a courtyard.
pois.notes: Cash only. Sells out by 15:00. The best mole negro I've eaten — period.

pois.name: ADO Bus Terminal
pois.description: First-class buses to Mexico City (6–7h overnight), Puerto Escondido (6h), and Puebla (5h).
pois.notes: Overnight bus to CDMX is the move — saves a night's accommodation.

cities.name: Tbilisi
cities.description: The Caucasus crossroads — Persian bathhouses, Soviet brutalism, Art Nouveau balconies, and the birthplace of wine all within walking distance.

pois.name: Narikala Fortress
pois.description: 4th-century fortress overlooking the old town — take the cable car up, walk down through sulfur bath district.
pois.notes: Golden hour from the ramparts is spectacular. Free entry.

pois.name: Dezerter Bazaar
pois.description: Chaotic covered market for churchkhela, tkemali, sulguni cheese, and chacha (grape vodka).
pois.notes: Cheese vendors will give you samples of everything. Arrive hungry.

pois.name: Abanotubani Sulfur Baths
pois.description: Domed Persian bathhouses fed by 37°C natural sulfur springs — Gulo's or Royal Baths for private rooms.
pois.notes: Book a private room (lari 40–60/hr). Ask for a kisi (scrub) treatment.

pois.name: Vino Underground
pois.description: Wine bar specialising in natural qvevri wines — Georgia invented wine 8,000 years ago; this is the best intro.
pois.notes: Ask for Rkatsiteli from Kakheti — the amber wine that changed my life.

pois.name: Tbilisi Central Station
pois.description: Night train to Baku (12h) and Yerevan (5h). Marshrutkas to Kazbegi (3h) from Didube.
pois.notes: For Kazbegi, take a marshrutka from Didube Metro — not the tourist minibus from Freedom Square.

cities.name: Chiang Mai
cities.description: Northern Thailand's rose of the north — 300 ancient Lanna temples, the world's best street food city (per Lonely Planet), and mountain trekking into Doi Inthanon.

pois.name: Doi Suthep Temple
pois.description: Golden chedi crowning a 1,676m mountain with panoramic views over the city.
pois.notes: Climb the 309-step Naga staircase rather than taking the tram. Views are better.

pois.name: Sunday Walking Street
pois.description: Wualai Road transforms into 1 km of street food — khao soi, sai ua sausage, mango sticky rice.
pois.notes: Sunday only, 17:00–23:00. Bring cash. The mango sticky rice near the gate is legendary.

pois.name: Doi Inthanon Trekking
pois.description: Thailand's highest peak at 2,565m — cloud forests, twin royal chedis, Karen hill tribe villages.
pois.notes: Hire a songthaew for the day (~฿1,500). The Ang Ka Nature Trail boardwalk is unmissable.

pois.name: Free Bird Café
pois.description: NGO café supporting Shan refugees — best real coffee in Chiang Mai, strong wi-fi, good vibes.
pois.notes: Try the shan noodles. All profits fund education for stateless children.

pois.name: Arcade Bus Terminal
pois.description: Long-distance hub — overnight buses to Bangkok (10h), Pai (3h), Chiang Rai (3h).
pois.notes: Nakhon Chai Air buses to Bangkok are the most reliable. Book at the terminal.

cities.name: Sarajevo
cities.description: Where East meets West — Ottoman bazaars run into Austro-Hungarian boulevards. A city that survived a 4-year siege and emerged with unforgettable hospitality.

pois.name: Baščaršija Bazaar
pois.description: 15th-century Ottoman bazaar — coppersmiths, jewellers, and the famous Sebilj fountain at its heart.
pois.notes: The Gazi Husrev-beg Mosque next door is open to visitors. Remove shoes.

pois.name: Ćevabdžinica Željo
pois.description: Sarajevo's best ćevapi — hand-minced lamb and beef sausages in a somun flatbread with kajmak.
pois.notes: Order 10 pieces minimum. The queue moves fast. Cash only.

pois.name: Yellow Fortress (Žuta Tabija)
pois.description: Ottoman lookout tower with the best sunset view over the red-roofed old town.
pois.notes: Walk up through the Jewish cemetery — sobering and beautiful. Free.

pois.name: War Tunnel Museum
pois.description: 800m tunnel dug under the airport runway during the 1992–95 siege — the city's lifeline.
pois.notes: Take a taxi (~15 KM). The owner's family still lives in the house above the tunnel entrance.

pois.name: Sarajevo Bus Station
pois.description: Buses to Mostar (2.5h), Dubrovnik (5h), Belgrade (6h), and Zagreb (8h).
pois.notes: Mostar day trip is unmissable — last bus back around 19:30.

cities.name: Cartagena
cities.description: Colombia's Caribbean jewel — pastel colonial mansions behind 13km of city walls, reggaeton drifting from every door, and the clearest water in South America a short boat ride away.

pois.name: Las Murallas (City Walls)
pois.description: 13 km of Spanish colonial walls built from 1586 — walk the entire circuit at golden hour.
pois.notes: Best sunset spot: Baluarte de San Francisco Javier. Vendedores sell cold coconut water.

pois.name: La Cevichería
pois.description: Anthony Bourdain's favourite — Colombian-style ceviche with coconut milk and ají amarillo.
pois.notes: Arrive when it opens. Queue forms fast after the Bourdain episode aired.

pois.name: Islas del Rosario
pois.description: National park archipelago — snorkelling over coral reefs in 30m-visibility Caribbean water.
pois.notes: Collective boat from Muelle Turístico (~COP 85,000 return). Book the day before.

pois.name: Café del Mar (sunset perch)
pois.description: Bar on top of the city walls — overpriced drinks, completely worth it for the Caribbean sunset.
pois.notes: The secret: buy a beer from a street vendor below and just stand on the wall. Same view, free.

pois.name: Terminal de Transportes
pois.description: Buses to Barranquilla (2h), Santa Marta (4h, for Tayrona park), and Medellin (13h overnight).
pois.notes: For Tayrona, take a bus to Santa Marta then a colectivo to the park entrance.
